title: calophyllum praetermissum
intro: Calophyllum praetermissum is a species of tree in the family Calophyllaceae, native to the island of New Caledonia, characterized by its large, flat leaves and fragrant wood. The medicinal properties of Calophyllum praetermissum include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and even exhibit anticancer properties. Calophyllum praetermissum is often used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, infusions, and extracts, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
